title: glycine soja subsp. formosana
intro: Glycine soja subsp. formosana, also known as Formosa Glycine, is a plant species within the legume family, native to Taiwan and China, and is a subspecies of the wild soybean. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to its rich content of isoflavones and other bioactive compounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease, improving bone density, and alleviating symptoms of menopause. Glycine soja subsp. formosana can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, capsules, and infusions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
